key_strengths: World's largest derivatives exchange with irreplaceable liquidity network effects — two-sided market self-reinforces against all competitors
Exceptional economics: 65% GAAP operating margin, near-100% FCF conversion, $83.5M capex on $6.5B revenue
Fortress balance sheet: net cash positive ($4.6B cash vs. $3.4B debt), 24x interest coverage, Aa3/AA- rated
Moat stress-tested: FMX volume collapsed >two-thirds during April 2025 tariff volatility as traders reverted to CME liquidity
~$20B cross-margining capital savings for clients creates profound switching costs that make competitor migration economically irrational
Four consecutive years of record revenue and adjusted EPS; 14.7% annualized 10-year return vs. 10.9% for S&P 500
One of the lowest-beta large-caps (~0.28) — genuine portfolio diversification benefit
key_risks: Sustained low-volatility macro regime (Likelihood: 30%): prolonged Fed stability would reduce interest rate derivatives volumes 20-30%, compressing earnings against fixed cost base
Valuation multiple compression (Likelihood: 40%): at 3.7% FCF yield, rising bond yields increase opportunity cost and could compress multiples 10-20% with no fundamental deterioration
FMX competitive erosion in rate futures (Likelihood: 20%): major-bank-backed competitor remains operational and could resume gaining share in SOFR/Treasury futures
Governance friction: persistent say-on-pay failures (ISS/Glass Lewis recommending against in multiple cycles), combined Chairman/CEO structure, CEO net selling of shares
Google Cloud migration execution risk: multi-year infrastructure transition creates operational vulnerability window
red_flags: Margin of safety score 4/10 — price offers no meaningful discount to base-case intrinsic value at $314
Analyst consensus price target ($260-$286) is 9-17% below current stock price
Upside/downside ratio below 1:1: bear downside (~38%) as large as bull upside (~40%) with unfavorable base-case probability weighting
Persistent say-on-pay failures with less than one-third approval in 2023 cycle; ISS and Glass Lewis both recommend against
CEO Terry Duffy net seller of 55,000 shares over past year with no purchases; combined Chairman/CEO role
buy_triggers: Stock retraces to $220-$250 (~20-30% below current), establishing genuine discount to base-case intrinsic value
Renewed Fed rate cycle (rate hikes or cuts after pause) drives ADV acceleration and earnings beats with modest price pullback restoring margin of safety
Crypto derivatives revenue reaches 10%+ of total revenue, signaling structural growth re-rating not in consensus estimates
sell_triggers: FMX or competitor captures >10% open interest in CME US rate futures complex over any 24-month window — moat fracture signal in the half of revenue dependent on rates
Three consecutive quarters of ADV decline not attributable to cyclical low-volatility environment — structural vs. cyclical volume deterioration
Valuation extreme: stock reaches P/owner-earnings above 35x (~$400+) in absence of accelerating underlying growth
valuation_summary: At ~$314, CME trades at ~27.7x P/owner-earnings and ~19.5x EV/EBITDA — modestly above its 10-year median of 17.9x; base-case perpetuity intrinsic value is approximately $270-$310, placing the stock at or slightly above fair value with no meaningful margin of safety; a buyer requires ~5-6% annual earnings growth in perpetuity to justify today's price, while bear-case DCF at $155 implies 51% downside if the macro environment shifts structurally toward sustained low volatility.
source_summary: Analysis is based on CME Group's FY2025 earnings press release (February 2026), Q4 2025 and Q4 2024 press releases, SEC proxy filings, Moody's and S&P credit rating affirmations (May 2025), GuruFocus ROIC/EV/EBITDA data, MacroTrends margin history, StockAnalysis.com, analyst consensus from FinancialContent/Barchart, and web searches for competitive data on FMX; all fundamental data is current as of February 2026 with stock price as of March 2026.
